learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate effective communication techniques to facilitate person-centred dental practice
Critically reflect on dental practice experiences understanding multiple perspectives and context
Explain the aetiology of major oral diseases and apply principles of minimal intervention dentistry
Demonstrate emerging manual dexterity skills applying the principles of minimal intervention dentistry
Conduct a comprehensive clinical examination of the oral cavity and associated structures in a simulated setting.
Apply frameworks to formulate evidence-based preventive management plans for dental caries.
Demonstrate an applied knowledge of direct restorative materials and their properties.

assessments.task_description:
This 60 min in-semester exam will cover material delivered in specific weeks, and may include multiple-choice, multiple-answer, or short-answer type questions. Inspera Assessment will be used for the assessment.

The in-semester exam is centrally organised and invigilated. The exam date will be communicated via UQ Examinations directly to the student.

  • Further details will be provided on the Blackboard site for this course at start of semester.
  • This is a digital exam conducted on Inspera platform and students in this course will need to complete and submit this using their own devices on site at a UQ location specified by UQ Central Examinations.
  • If you do not have a laptop, you may be able to borrow a laptop from the Library (https://web.library.uq.edu.au/library-services/it/laptop-loans)
assessments.submission_guidelines: The exam will be completed in Inspera Assessment using Safe Exam Browser (SEB) Please ensure that you have already downloaded Safe Exam Browser (SEB) to your computer. You can do this by visiting https://web.library.uq.edu.au/library-services/it/learnuq-blackboard-help/inspera-assessment/safe-exam-browser-inspera and completing the steps to install the application (Windows or MacOS) and test it on your computer by completing a preparation test. On exam day you will be provided with instructions on how to access your exam via the Safe Exam Browser (SEB) application.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: There is no late submission allowed for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The purpose of this assessment is to evaluate the student’s pre-clinical/peer clinic performance at the interim stage and provide formative feedback to support improvement before the final clinical competence assessment at the end of semester.

Course Coordinator in consultation with the pre-clinical/peer clinic supervisory team will complete a report based on student’s clinical performance during the assessment period and quality of critical self-reflections following each session.

See Submission Guidelines for all components that must be submitted for the interim clinical practice performance task to be assessed. Detailed assessment criteria and rubrics will be provided on Blackboard.

Two domains will be assessed to an expectations met (EM) or not met (ENM) standard and mapped to the Professional competency requirements for the accreditation body for this program - The Australian Dental Council (Professional Competencies of the newly Qualified Dentist)

The domains being assessed are: 

1. Professionalism, Communication and Health Promotion 

2. Clinical Treatment and Evaluation

To achieve a pass, the criteria are as follows:

All domains are weighted equally, and the student must achieve a pass in each domain, and an overall pass.

  • A pass in each domain will involve a student receiving 3 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark in Domain 1, and no more than 3 "expectation not met" (ENM) in Domains 2. 
  • An overall pass for the report will involve a student receiving 6 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark across the 2 domains.


Please note:

  • Written feedback will be provided for any areas to improve as this is formative. Students must reflect on feedback and demonstrate improvement in the latter half of the semester.
  • Logbooks may be randomly audited by the Course Coordinator for authenticity and supervisor signatures.
  • Any significant clinical concerns related to safety will be escalated immediately via course coordinator pathways and documented and taken into consideration for the assessment outcomes.


This is a formative assessment to enable the student to receive feedback on their developing clinical competency.

Any significant clinical concerns pertaining to patient safety will be escalated via clinical communication pathways to the course coordinator and recorded on this report to be considered for the assessment outcome.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Detailed marking rubric for this assessment is available on Blackboard. See Submission Guidelines for all components that must be submitted for the portfolio to be assessed for this clinical practice performance task.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment task takes the form of cavity preparation tasks using equipment/instrumentation and the Typodont model. Students will be assessed on the performance to demonstrate satisfactory performance of manual dexterity skills and handpiece handling. The practical assessment will be conducted in the laboratory setting under timed conditions during a designated class session. Students will be given time to set up for the practical assessment in the laboratory setting.

The assessment must be completed unassisted by supervisors; students have sixty(60) minutes to complete a single (1) preparation in their Typodont model, on the indicated tooth number (e.g. tooth 36) according to supplied specifications, while demonstrating a consistently high standard of laboratory PPE compliance, handpiece handling, and safe operations.

Students will have opportunities to practise cavity preparation techniques and will be able to receive feedback for practice from supervisors during the semester, and leading up to the assessment.

This assessment task is not eligible for a re-mark as it will be assessed by two examiners and post-marking moderation process will be applied.
assessments.submission_guidelines: After the 60 minutes allowed to complete the specified preparation, students will leave the venue while examiners evaluate the work presented. When marking is completed and models returned, students may seek individual feedback on their performance from examiners once they collect their Typodont model.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: There is no late submission allowed for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The purpose of this assessment is to evaluate the student’s pre-clinical and peer clinic performance for this course, at the end of the semester. Course Coordinator in consultation with the pre-clinical/peer clinic supervisory team will complete a report based on student’s pre-clinical /peer clinic performance during the assessment period and quality of critical self-reflections following each session.

Students are advised to review any feedback provided in the interim report, to improve pre-clinical/peer clinic performance, in the latter part of his course, to pass this final clinical practice performance assessment which is must pass to pass this course.

Detailed marking rubric for this assessment is available on Blackboard.

See Submission Guidelines for all components that must be submitted for this clinical practice performance task to be assessed. The submitted portfolio will be reviewed and performance in each domain considered and used to complete a report of overall performance in each domain as per the criteria and standards.

The clinical portfolio will be reviewed and performance in each domain considered and used to complete a report of overall performance in each domain.

Two domains will be assessed to an expectations met (EM) or not met (ENM) standard and mapped to the professional competency requirements for the accreditation body for this program - The Australian Dental Council (Professional Competencies of the newly Qualified Dentist)

The domains being assessed are: 

1. Professionalism, Communication and Health Promotion 

2. Clinical Treatment and Evaluation

Passing requirements:

To pass this assessment, students must achieve a pass on this summative assessment on clinical performance progress at this stage. To achieve a pass, the criteria are as follows:

All domains are weighted equally, and the student must achieve a pass in each domain, and an overall pass.

  • A pass in each domain will involve a student receiving 3 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark in Domain 1, and no more than 3 "expectation not met" (ENM) in Domain 2. 
  • An overall pass for the report will involve a student receiving 6 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark across the 2 domains.


Students must complete all required components as outlined in Submission Guidelines for the portfolio submission.

Please note:

  • Written feedback provided for any areas to improve from the interim task, must be reflected on and student should demonstrate improvement in the latter half of the semester to enable passing of this must pass task.
  • Logbooks may be randomly audited by the Course Coordinator for authenticity and supervisor signatures.
  • Any significant clinical concerns related to safety will be escalated immediately via course coordinator communication pathways and documented in the report and taken into consideration for the final assessment outcome.


To pass this assessment item, students must actively participate in a minimum of 90% pre-clinic sessions and 90% peer-clinic sessions as per published rosters and timetables. All allocated tasks in each session must be completed successfully and allocated clinical educator must deem them to be satisfactory.

In accordance with ﾠPPL Assessment Procedure Section 3 Part J,ﾠsupplementary assessment will not be available for this final clinical practice performance task, as professional competencies are developed over a 13-week practicum.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The submitted portfolio must be COMPLETE in all the following respects and submitted by the published due date which will then be assessed as part of this assessment task. Any incomplete or missing information can result in a fail grade in this must pass assessment task.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
A sixty (60) minute examination containing multiple choice and short answer questions covering all of the course learning material delivered for a possible sixty (60) marks. This is a MUST PASS assessment item (achieve a minimum of 50% to pass).

Learning material covered in the examination:

  • Principles of prevention in dental practice
  • Non-carious tooth loss
  • Dental caries: aetiology, clinical and radiographic identification, risk assessment and classification, conservative management
  • Moisture control and isolation
  • Occlusal analysis for restoration
  • Dental debridement
  • Principles of cavity preparation
  • Intracoronal dental restorative materials, selection rationale, related restorative armamentarium
  • Dental Biomaterials
assessments.submission_guidelines: The exam will be completed in Inspera Assessment using Safe Exam Browser (SEB) Please ensure that you have already downloaded Safe Exam Browser (SEB) to your computer. You can do this by visiting https://web.library.uq.edu.au/library-services/it/learnuq-blackboard-help/inspera-assessment/safe-exam-browser-inspera and completing the steps to install the application (Windows or MacOS) and test it on your computer by completing a preparation test. On exam day you will be provided with instructions on how to access your exam via the Safe Exam Browser (SEB) application.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Clinical Coaching Sessions (F2F)

Learning sessions includes various topics, such as: etiology/investigation/diagnosis of caries, management of caries including removal, rationale and application of isolation, restorative materials, etc.

Further detailed timetables will be available on the Blackboard course site.
learning_activities.topic_description: Clinical Coaching Sessions (F2F) Learning sessions includes various topics, such as: etiology/investigation/diagnosis of caries, management of caries including removal, rationale and application of isolation, restorative materials, etc. Further detailed timetables will be available on the Blackboard course site.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Pre-clinic

A series of sessions in the preclinical simulation laboratory will be delivered: safe dental practice, introduction to restorative equipment and instruments, caries management based practical sessions, application techniques for restorative materials.

Further detailed timetables will be available on the Blackboard course site. Attendance is required.
learning_activities.topic_description: Pre-clinic A series of sessions in the preclinical simulation laboratory will be delivered: safe dental practice, introduction to restorative equipment and instruments, caries management based practical sessions, application techniques for restorative materials. Further detailed timetables will be available on the Blackboard course site. Attendance is required.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Peer Clinic

Clinical activities/training sessions: students will be expected to apply their theoretical knowledge and practical skills learnt throughout the coaching and pre-clinic session into the clinical sessions, under the supervision of a dental practitioner. Clinical learning will be recorded in a log book throughout the semester. Attendance is required.
learning_activities.topic_description: Peer Clinic Clinical activities/training sessions: students will be expected to apply their theoretical knowledge and practical skills learnt throughout the coaching and pre-clinic session into the clinical sessions, under the supervision of a dental practitioner. Clinical learning will be recorded in a log book throughout the semester. Attendance is required.
